title: euphrasia gracilis
intro: Euphrasia gracilis, also known as Grass-of-Parnassus, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, making it a popular remedy for eye infections and respiratory issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of alkaloids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, contributing to its health benefits, such as improving vision, reducing inflammation, and boosting the immune system. Euphrasia gracilis can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or ointment, and is often used in herbal medicine to treat conditions such as conjunctivitis, bronchitis, and sinusitis.
